NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. A Song. Let God arise, Let His enemies be scattered; Let those also who hate Him flee before Him.
NKJV.2: As smoke is driven away, So drive them away; As wax melts before the fire, So let the wicked perish at the presence of God.
NKJV.3: But let the righteous be glad; Let them rejoice before God; Yes, let them rejoice exceedingly.
NKJV.4: Sing to God, sing praises to His name;  Extol Him who rides on the clouds,  By His name YAH, And rejoice before Him.
NKJV.5: A father of the fatherless, a defender of widows, Is God in His holy habitation.
NKJV.6: God sets the solitary in families; He brings out those who are bound into prosperity; But the rebellious dwell in a dry land.
NKJV.7: O God, when You went out before Your people, When You marched through the wilderness, Selah
NKJV.8: The earth shook; The heavens also dropped rain at the presence of God; Sinai itself was moved at the presence of God, the God of Israel.
NKJV.9: You, O God, sent a plentiful rain, Whereby You confirmed Your inheritance, When it was weary.
NKJV.10: Your congregation dwelt in it;  You, O God, provided from Your goodness for the poor.
NKJV.11: The Lord gave the word; Great was the company of those who proclaimed it:
NKJV.12: “Kings  of armies flee, they flee, And she who remains at home divides the spoil.
NKJV.13: Though you lie down among the sheepfolds, You will be like the wings of a dove covered with silver, And her feathers with yellow gold.”
NKJV.14: When the Almighty scattered kings in it, It was white as snow in Zalmon.
NKJV.15: A mountain of God is the mountain of Bashan; A mountain of many peaks is the mountain of Bashan.
NKJV.16: Why do you fume with envy, you mountains of many peaks? This is the mountain which God desires to dwell in; Yes, the LORD will dwell in it forever.
NKJV.17: The chariots of God are twenty thousand, Even thousands of thousands; The Lord is among them as in Sinai, in the Holy Place.
NKJV.18: You have ascended on high, You have led captivity captive; You have received gifts among men, Even from the rebellious, That the LORD God might dwell there.
NKJV.19: Blessed be the Lord, Who daily loads us with benefits, The God of our salvation! Selah
NKJV.20: Our God is the God of salvation; And to GOD the Lord belong escapes from death.
NKJV.21: But God will wound the head of His enemies,  The hairy scalp of the one who still goes on in his trespasses.
NKJV.22: The Lord said, “I will bring back from Bashan, I will bring them back from the depths of the sea,
NKJV.23: That your foot may crush them in blood, And the tongues of your dogs may have their portion from your enemies.”
NKJV.24: They have seen Your procession, O God, The procession of my God, my King, into the sanctuary.
NKJV.25: The singers went before, the players on instruments followed after; Among them were the maidens playing timbrels.
NKJV.26: Bless God in the congregations, The Lord, from the fountain of Israel.
NKJV.27: There is little Benjamin, their leader, The princes of Judah and their company, The princes of Zebulun and the princes of Naphtali.
NKJV.28: Your God has commanded your strength; Strengthen, O God, what You have done for us.
NKJV.29: Because of Your temple at Jerusalem,  Kings will bring presents to You.
NKJV.30: Rebuke the beasts of the reeds, The herd of bulls with the calves of the peoples, Till everyone submits himself with pieces of silver. Scatter the peoples who delight in war.
NKJV.31: Envoys will come out of Egypt;  Ethiopia will quickly stretch out her hands to God.
NKJV.32: Sing to God, you kingdoms of the earth; Oh, sing praises to the Lord, Selah
NKJV.33: To Him who rides on the heaven of heavens, which were of old! Indeed, He sends out His voice, a mighty voice.
NKJV.34: Ascribe strength to God; His excellence is over Israel, And His strength is in the clouds.
NKJV.35: O God, You are more awesome than Your holy places. The God of Israel is He who gives strength and power to His people. Blessed be God!

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> or Song<S>7892</S> of David<S>1732</S>. Let God<S>430</S> arise<S>6965</S>, let his enemies<S>341</S> be scattered<S>6327</S>: let them also that hate<S>8130</S> him flee<S>5127</S> before<S>6440</S> him. <sup>before: Heb. from his face</sup>

KJV.9: Thou, O God<S>430</S>, didst send<S>5130</S> a plentiful<S>5071</S> rain<S>1653</S>, whereby thou didst confirm<S>3559</S> thine inheritance<S>5159</S>, when it was weary<S>3811</S>. <sup>send: Heb. shake out</sup> <sup>confirm: Heb. confirm it</sup>

KJV.11: The Lord<S>136</S> gave<S>5414</S> the word<S>562</S>: great<S>7227</S> was the company<S>6635</S> of those that published<S>1319</S> it. <sup>company: Heb. army</sup>

KJV.24: They have seen<S>7200</S> thy goings<S>1979</S>, O God<S>430</S>; even the goings<S>1979</S> of my God<S>410</S>, my King<S>4428</S>, in the sanctuary<S>6944</S>.

ESV.1: God shall arise, his enemies shall be scattered; and those who hate him shall flee before him!
ESV.2: As smoke is driven away, so you shall drive them away; as wax melts before fire, so the wicked shall perish before God!
ESV.3: But the righteous shall be glad; they shall exult before God; they shall be jubilant with joy!
ESV.4: Sing to God, sing praises to his name; lift up a song to him who rides through the deserts; his name is the LORD; exult before him!
ESV.5: Father of the fatherless and protector of widows is God in his holy habitation.
ESV.6: God settles the solitary in a home; he leads out the prisoners to prosperity, but the rebellious dwell in a parched land.
ESV.7: O God, when you went out before your people, when you marched through the wilderness, Selah
ESV.8: the earth quaked, the heavens poured down rain, before God, the One of Sinai, before God,  the God of Israel.
ESV.9: Rain in abundance, O God, you shed abroad; you restored your inheritance as it languished;
ESV.10: your flock  found a dwelling in it; in your goodness, O God, you provided for the needy.
ESV.11: The Lord gives the word; the women who announce the news are a great host:
ESV.12: “The kings of the armies — they flee, they flee!” The women at home divide the spoil —
ESV.13: though you men lie among the sheepfolds — the wings of a dove covered with silver, its pinions with shimmering gold.
ESV.14: When the Almighty scatters kings there, let snow fall on Zalmon.
ESV.15: O mountain of God, mountain of Bashan; O many-peaked  mountain, mountain of Bashan!
ESV.16: Why do you look with hatred, O many-peaked mountain, at the mount that God desired for his abode, yes, where the LORD will dwell forever?
ESV.17: The chariots of God are twice ten thousand, thousands upon thousands; the Lord is among them; Sinai is now in the sanctuary.
ESV.18: You ascended on high, leading a host of captives in your train and receiving gifts among men, even among the rebellious, that the LORD God may dwell there.
ESV.19: Blessed be the Lord, who daily bears us up; God is our salvation. Selah
ESV.20: Our God is a God of salvation, and to GOD, the Lord, belong deliverances from death.
ESV.21: But God will strike the heads of his enemies, the hairy crown of him who walks in his guilty ways.
ESV.22: The Lord said, “I will bring them back from Bashan, I will bring them back from the depths of the sea,
ESV.23: that you may strike your feet in their blood, that the tongues of your dogs may have their portion from the foe.”
ESV.24: Your procession is  seen, O God, the procession of my God, my King, into the sanctuary —
ESV.25: the singers in front, the musicians last, between them virgins playing tambourines:
ESV.26: “Bless God in the great congregation, the LORD, O you  who are of Israel's fountain!”
ESV.27: There is Benjamin, the least of them, in the lead, the princes of Judah in their throng, the princes of Zebulun, the princes of Naphtali.
ESV.28: Summon your power, O God,  the power, O God, by which you have worked for us.
ESV.29: Because of your temple at Jerusalem kings shall bear gifts to you.
ESV.30: Rebuke the beasts that dwell among the reeds, the herd of bulls with the calves of the peoples. Trample underfoot those who lust after tribute; scatter the peoples who delight in war.
ESV.31: Nobles shall come from Egypt; Cush shall hasten to stretch out her hands to God.
ESV.32: O kingdoms of the earth, sing to God; sing praises to the Lord, Selah
ESV.33: to him who rides in the heavens, the ancient heavens; behold, he sends out his voice, his mighty voice.
ESV.34: Ascribe power to God, whose majesty is over Israel, and whose power is in the skies.
ESV.35: Awesome is God from his  sanctuary; the God of Israel — he is the one who gives power and strength to his people. Blessed be God!

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Psalm of David. A Song. Let God arise, let His enemies be scattered, And let those who hate Him flee before Him.
NASB.2: As smoke is driven away, [so] drive [them] away; As wax melts before the fire, [So] let the wicked perish before God.
NASB.3: But let the righteous be glad; let them exult before God; Yes, let them rejoice with gladness.
NASB.4: Sing to God, sing praises to His name; Lift up [a song] for Him who rides through the deserts, Whose name is the LORD, and exult before Him.
NASB.5: A father of the fatherless and a judge for the widows, Is God in His holy habitation.
NASB.6: God makes a home for the lonely; He leads out the prisoners into prosperity, Only the rebellious dwell in a parched land.
NASB.7: O God, when You went forth before Your people, When You marched through the wilderness, Selah.
NASB.8: The earth quaked; The heavens also dropped [rain] at the presence of God; Sinai itself [quaked] at the presence of God, the God of Israel.
NASB.9: You shed abroad a plentiful rain, O God; You confirmed Your inheritance when it was parched.
NASB.10: Your creatures settled in it; You provided in Your goodness for the poor, O God.
NASB.11: The Lord gives the command; The women who proclaim the [good] tidings are a great host:
NASB.12: 'Kings of armies flee, they flee, And she who remains at home will divide the spoil!'
NASB.13: When you lie down among the sheepfolds, [You are like] the wings of a dove covered with silver, And its pinions with glistening gold.
NASB.14: When the Almighty scattered the kings there, It was snowing in Zalmon.
NASB.15: A mountain of God is the mountain of Bashan; A mountain [of many] peaks is the mountain of Bashan.
NASB.16: Why do you look with envy, O mountains with [many] peaks, At the mountain which God has desired for His abode? Surely the LORD will dwell [there] forever.
NASB.17: The chariots of God are myriads, thousands upon thousands; The Lord is among them [as at] Sinai, in holiness.
NASB.18: You have ascended on high, You have led captive [Your] captives; You have received gifts among men, Even [among] the rebellious also, that the LORD God may dwell [there].
NASB.19: Blessed be the Lord, who daily bears our burden, The God [who] is our salvation. Selah.
NASB.20: God is to us a God of deliverances; And to GOD the Lord belong escapes from death.
NASB.21: Surely God will shatter the head of His enemies, The hairy crown of him who goes on in his guilty deeds.
NASB.22: The Lord said, 'I will bring [them] back from Bashan. I will bring [them] back from the depths of the sea;
NASB.23: That your foot may shatter [them] in blood, The tongue of your dogs [may have] its portion from [your] enemies.'
NASB.24: They have seen Your procession, O God, The procession of my God, my King, into the sanctuary.
NASB.25: The singers went on, the musicians after [them], In the midst of the maidens beating tambourines.
NASB.26: Bless God in the congregations, [Even] the LORD, [you who are] of the fountain of Israel.
NASB.27: There is Benjamin, the youngest, ruling them, The princes of Judah [in] their throng, The princes of Zebulun, the princes of Naphtali.
NASB.28: Your God has commanded your strength; Show Yourself strong, O God, who have acted on our behalf.
NASB.29: Because of Your temple at Jerusalem Kings will bring gifts to You.
NASB.30: Rebuke the beasts in the reeds, The herd of bulls with the calves of the peoples, Trampling under foot the pieces of silver; He has scattered the peoples who delight in war.
NASB.31: Envoys will come out of Egypt; Ethiopia will quickly stretch out her hands to God.
NASB.32: Sing to God, O kingdoms of the earth, Sing praises to the Lord, Selah.
NASB.33: To Him who rides upon the highest heavens, which are from ancient times; Behold, He speaks forth with His voice, a mighty voice.
NASB.34: Ascribe strength to God; His majesty is over Israel And His strength is in the skies.
NASB.35: O God, [You are] awesome from Your sanctuary. The God of Israel Himself gives strength and power to the people. Blessed be God!

NLT.1:
Rise up, O God, and scatter your enemies.
Let those who hate God run for their lives.
NLT.2:
Blow them away like smoke.
Melt them like wax in a fire.
Let the wicked perish in the presence of God.
NLT.3:
But let the godly rejoice.
Let them be glad in God’s presence.
Let them be filled with joy.
NLT.4:
Sing praises to God and to his name!
Sing loud praises to him who rides the clouds.
His name is the LORD —
rejoice in his presence!
NLT.5:
Father to the fatherless, defender of widows —
this is God, whose dwelling is holy.
NLT.6:
God places the lonely in families;
he sets the prisoners free and gives them joy.
But he makes the rebellious live in a sun-scorched land.
NLT.7:
O God, when you led your people out from Egypt,
when you marched through the dry wasteland, Interlude
NLT.8:
the earth trembled, and the heavens poured down rain
before you, the God of Sinai,
before God, the God of Israel.
NLT.9:
You sent abundant rain, O God,
to refresh the weary land.
NLT.10:
There your people finally settled,
and with a bountiful harvest, O God,
you provided for your needy people.
NLT.11:
The Lord gives the word,
and a great army brings the good news.
NLT.12:
Enemy kings and their armies flee,
while the women of Israel divide the plunder.
NLT.13:
Even those who lived among the sheepfolds found treasures —
doves with wings of silver
and feathers of gold.
NLT.14:
The Almighty scattered the enemy kings
like a blowing snowstorm on Mount Zalmon.
NLT.15:
The mountains of Bashan are majestic,
with many peaks stretching high into the sky.
NLT.16:
Why do you look with envy, O rugged mountains,
at Mount Zion, where God has chosen to live,
where the LORD himself will live forever?
NLT.17:
Surrounded by unnumbered thousands of chariots,
the Lord came from Mount Sinai into his sanctuary.
NLT.18:
When you ascended to the heights,
you led a crowd of captives.
You received gifts from the people,
even from those who rebelled against you.
Now the LORD God will live among us there.
NLT.19:
Praise the Lord; praise God our savior!
For each day he carries us in his arms. Interlude
NLT.20:
Our God is a God who saves!
The Sovereign LORD rescues us from death.
NLT.21:
But God will smash the heads of his enemies,
crushing the skulls of those who love their guilty ways.
NLT.22:
The Lord says, “I will bring my enemies down from Bashan;
I will bring them up from the depths of the sea.
NLT.23:
You, my people, will wash your feet in their blood,
and even your dogs will get their share!”
NLT.24:
Your procession has come into view, O God —
the procession of my God and King as he goes into the sanctuary.
NLT.25:
Singers are in front, musicians behind;
between them are young women playing tambourines.
NLT.26:
Praise God, all you people of Israel;
praise the LORD, the source of Israel’s life.
NLT.27:
Look, the little tribe of Benjamin leads the way.
Then comes a great throng of rulers from Judah
and all the rulers of Zebulun and Naphtali.
NLT.28:
Summon your might, O God.
Display your power, O God, as you have in the past.
NLT.29:
The kings of the earth are bringing tribute
to your Temple in Jerusalem.
NLT.30:
Rebuke these enemy nations —
these wild animals lurking in the reeds,
this herd of bulls among the weaker calves.
Make them bring bars of silver in humble tribute.
Scatter the nations that delight in war.
NLT.31:
Let Egypt come with gifts of precious metals ;
let Ethiopia bring tribute to God.
NLT.32:
Sing to God, you kingdoms of the earth.
Sing praises to the Lord. Interlude
NLT.33:
Sing to the one who rides across the ancient heavens,
his mighty voice thundering from the sky.
NLT.34:
Tell everyone about God’s power.
His majesty shines down on Israel;
his strength is mighty in the heavens.
NLT.35:
God is awesome in his sanctuary.
The God of Israel gives power and strength to his people.
Praise be to God!

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. A song. Let God arise! Let his enemies be scattered! Let them who hate him also flee before him.
WEB.2: As smoke is driven away, so drive them away. As wax melts before the fire, so let the wicked perish at the presence of God.
WEB.3: But let the righteous be glad. Let them rejoice before God. Yes, let them rejoice with gladness.
WEB.4: Sing to God! Sing praises to his name! Extol him who rides on the clouds: to Yah, his name! Rejoice before him!
WEB.5: A father of the fatherless, and a defender of the widows, is God in his holy habitation.
WEB.6: God sets the lonely in families. He brings out the prisoners with singing, but the rebellious dwell in a sun-scorched land.
WEB.7: God, when you went out before your people, when you marched through the wilderness... Selah.
WEB.8: The earth trembled. The sky also poured down rain at the presence of the God of Sinai — at the presence of God, the God of Israel.
WEB.9: You, God, sent a plentiful rain. You confirmed your inheritance, when it was weary.
WEB.10: Your congregation lived therein. You, God, prepared your goodness for the poor.
WEB.11: The Lord announced the word. The ones who proclaim it are a great company.
WEB.12: “Kings of armies flee! They flee!” She who waits at home divides the plunder,
WEB.13: while you sleep among the camp fires, the wings of a dove sheathed with silver, her feathers with shining gold.
WEB.14: When the Almighty scattered kings in her, it snowed on Zalmon.
WEB.15: The mountains of Bashan are majestic mountains. The mountains of Bashan are rugged.
WEB.16: Why do you look in envy, you rugged mountains, at the mountain where God chooses to reign? Yes, Yahweh will dwell there forever.
WEB.17: The chariots of God are tens of thousands and thousands of thousands. The Lord is among them, from Sinai, into the sanctuary.
WEB.18: You have ascended on high. You have led away captives. You have received gifts among men, yes, among the rebellious also, that Yah God might dwell there.
WEB.19: Blessed be the Lord, who daily bears our burdens, even the God who is our salvation. Selah.
WEB.20: God is to us a God of deliverance. To Yahweh, the Lord, belongs escape from death.
WEB.21: But God will strike through the head of his enemies, the hairy scalp of such a one as still continues in his guiltiness.
WEB.22: The Lord said, “I will bring you again from Bashan, I will bring you again from the depths of the sea;
WEB.23: That you may crush them, dipping your foot in blood, that the tongues of your dogs may have their portion from your enemies.”
WEB.24: They have seen your processions, God, even the processions of my God, my King, into the sanctuary.
WEB.25: The singers went before, the minstrels followed after, among the ladies playing with tambourines,
WEB.26: “Bless God in the congregations, even the Lord in the assembly of Israel!”
WEB.27: There is little Benjamin, their ruler, the princes of Judah, their council, the princes of Zebulun, and the princes of Naphtali.
WEB.28: Your God has commanded your strength. Strengthen, God, that which you have done for us.
WEB.29: Because of your temple at Jerusalem, kings shall bring presents to you.
WEB.30: Rebuke the wild animal of the reeds, the multitude of the bulls, with the calves of the peoples. Being humbled, may it bring bars of silver. Scatter the nations that delight in war.
WEB.31: Princes shall come out of Egypt. Ethiopia shall hurry to stretch out her hands to God.
WEB.32: Sing to God, you kingdoms of the earth! Sing praises to the Lord! Selah.
WEB.33: To him who rides on the heaven of heavens, which are of old; behold, he utters his voice, a mighty voice.
WEB.34: Ascribe strength to God! His excellency is over Israel, his strength is in the skies.
WEB.35: You are awesome, God, in your sanctuaries. The God of Israel gives strength and power to his people. Praise be to God!

CSB17.1:
For the choir director. A psalm of David. A song. [1] God arises. His enemies scatter,
and those who hate him flee from his presence. ⓙ
CSB17.2:
As smoke is blown away,
so you blow them away.
As wax melts before the fire,
so the wicked are destroyed before God. ⓚ
CSB17.3:
But the righteous are glad;
they rejoice before God and celebrate with joy. ⓛ
CSB17.4:
Sing to God! Sing praises to his name.
Exalt him who rides on the clouds [1]  — 
his name is the LORD [2]  — and celebrate before him. ⓜ
CSB17.5:
God in his holy dwelling is
a father of the fatherless
and a champion of widows. ⓝ
CSB17.6:
God provides homes for those who are deserted.
He leads out the prisoners to prosperity, [3]
but the rebellious live in a scorched land. ⓐ
CSB17.7:
God, when you went out before your people,
when you marched through the desert, Selah
CSB17.8:
the earth trembled and the skies poured rain
before God, the God of Sinai, [4]
before God, the God of Israel. ⓑ
CSB17.9:
You, God, showered abundant rain;
you revived your inheritance when it languished. ⓒ
CSB17.10:
Your people settled in it;
God, you provided for the poor by your goodness. ⓓ
CSB17.11:
The Lord gave the command;
a great company of women brought the good news: ⓔ
CSB17.12:
“The kings of the armies flee — they flee!”
She who stays at home divides the spoil. ⓕ
CSB17.13:
While [5] you lie among the sheep pens, [6]
the wings of a dove are covered with silver,
and its feathers with glistening gold. ⓖ
CSB17.14:
When the Almighty scattered kings in the land,
it snowed on Zalmon. [7] ⓗ
CSB17.15:
Mount Bashan is God’s towering mountain;
Mount Bashan is a mountain of many peaks. ⓘ
CSB17.16:
Why gaze with envy, you mountain peaks,
at the mountain God desired for his abode?
The LORD will dwell there forever! ⓙ
CSB17.17:
God’s chariots are tens of thousands,
thousands and thousands; ⓚ
the Lord is among them in the sanctuary [8]
as he was at Sinai. ⓛ
CSB17.18:
You ascended to the heights, taking away captives;
you received gifts from [9] people,
even from the rebellious,
so that the LORD God might dwell there. [10] ⓜ
CSB17.19:
Blessed be the Lord!
Day after day he bears our burdens;
God is our salvation. ⓝ Selah
CSB17.20:
Our God is a God of salvation,
and escape from death belongs to the LORD my Lord. ⓐ
CSB17.21:
Surely God crushes the heads of his enemies,
the hairy brow of one who goes on in his guilty acts. ⓑ
CSB17.22:
The Lord said, “I will bring them back from Bashan;
I will bring them back from the depths of the sea ⓒ
CSB17.23:
so that your foot may wade [11] in blood
and your dogs’ tongues may have their share
from the enemies.” ⓓ
CSB17.24:
People have seen your procession, God,
the procession of my God,
my King, in the sanctuary. ⓔ
CSB17.25:
Singers lead the way,
with musicians following;
among them are young women
playing tambourines. ⓕ
CSB17.26:
Bless God in the assemblies;
bless the LORD from the fountain of Israel. ⓖ
CSB17.27:
There is Benjamin, the youngest, leading them,
the rulers of Judah in their assembly, [12]
the rulers of Zebulun, the rulers of Naphtali. ⓗ
CSB17.28:
Your God has decreed your strength.
Show your strength, God,
you who have acted on our behalf. ⓘ
CSB17.29:
Because of your temple at Jerusalem,
kings will bring tribute to you. ⓙ
CSB17.30:
Rebuke the beast in the reeds,
the herd of bulls with the calves of the peoples. ⓚ
Trample underfoot those with bars of silver. [13]
Scatter the peoples who take pleasure in war. ⓛ
CSB17.31:
Ambassadors will come [14] from Egypt;
Cush will stretch out its hands to God. ⓜ
CSB17.32:
Sing to God, you kingdoms of the earth;
sing praise to the Lord, ⓝ Selah
CSB17.33:
to him who rides in the ancient, highest heavens. ⓐ
Look, he thunders with his powerful voice! ⓑ
CSB17.34:
Ascribe power to God.
His majesty is over Israel,
his power is among the clouds. ⓒ
CSB17.35:
God, you are awe-inspiring in your sanctuaries.
The God of Israel gives power and strength to his people.
Blessed be God! ⓓ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. A Song. Let God arise, and His enemies be scattered;
Let those who hate Him flee before Him.
AMP.2:
As smoke is driven away, so drive them away;
As wax melts before the fire,
So let the wicked and guilty perish before [the presence of] God.
AMP.3:
But let the righteous be glad; let them be in good spirits before God,
Yes, let them rejoice with delight.
AMP.4:
Sing to God, sing praises to His name;
Lift up a song for Him who rides through the desert—
His name is the LORD—be in good spirits before Him.
AMP.5:
A father of the fatherless and a judge and protector of the widows,
Is God in His holy habitation.
AMP.6:
God makes a home for the lonely;
He leads the prisoners into prosperity,
Only the stubborn and rebellious dwell in a parched land.
AMP.7:
O God, when You went out before Your people,
When You marched through the wilderness, Selah.
AMP.8:
The earth trembled;
The heavens also poured down rain at the presence of God;
Sinai itself trembled at the presence of God, the God of Israel.
AMP.9:
You, O God, sent abroad plentiful rain;
You confirmed Your inheritance when it was parched and weary.
AMP.10:
Your flock found a dwelling place in it;
O God, in Your goodness You provided for the poor.
AMP.11:
The Lord gives the command [to take Canaan];
The women who proclaim the good news are a great host (army);
AMP.12:
"The kings of the [enemies'] armies flee, they flee,
And the beautiful woman who remains at home divides the spoil [left behind]."
AMP.13:
When you lie down [to rest] among the sheepfolds,
You [Israel] are like the wings of a dove [of victory] overlaid with silver,
Its feathers glistening with gold [trophies taken from the enemy].
AMP.14:
When the Almighty scattered [the Canaanite] kings in the land of Canaan,
It was snowing on Zalmon.
AMP.15:
A mountain of God is the mountain of Bashan;
A [high] mountain of many summits is Mount Bashan [rising east of the Jordan].
AMP.16:
Why do you look with envy, mountains with many peaks,
At the mountain [of the city of Zion] which God has desired for His dwelling place?
Yes, the LORD will dwell there forever.
AMP.17:
The chariots of God are myriads, thousands upon thousands;
The Lord is among them as He was at Sinai, in holiness.
AMP.18:
You have ascended on high, You have led away captive Your captives;
You have received gifts among men,
Even from the rebellious also, that the LORD God may dwell there.
AMP.19:
Blessed be the Lord, who bears our burden day by day,
The God who is our salvation! Selah.
AMP.20:
God is to us a God of acts of salvation;
And to GOD the Lord belong escapes from death [setting us free].
AMP.21:
Surely God will shatter the head of His enemies,
The hairy scalp of one who goes on in his guilty ways.
AMP.22:
The Lord said, "I will bring your enemies back from Bashan;
I will bring them back from the depths of the [Red] Sea,
AMP.23:
That your foot may crush them in blood,
That the tongue of your dogs may have its share from your enemies."
AMP.24:
They have seen Your [solemn] procession, O God,
The procession of my God, my King, into the sanctuary [in holiness].
AMP.25:
The singers go in front, the players of instruments last;
Between them the maidens playing on tambourines.
AMP.26:
Bless God in the congregations, [give thanks, gratefully praise Him],
The LORD, you who are from [Jacob] the fountain of Israel.
AMP.27:
The youngest is there, Benjamin, ruling them,
The princes of Judah and their company [the southern tribes],
The princes of Zebulun and the princes of Naphtali [the northern tribes].
AMP.28:
Your God has commanded your strength [your power in His service and your resistance to temptation];
Show Yourself strong, O God, who acted on our behalf.
AMP.29:
Because of Your temple at Jerusalem
[Pagan] kings will bring gifts to You [out of respect].
AMP.30:
Rebuke the beasts [living] among the reeds [in Egypt],
The herd of bulls (the leaders) with the calves of the peoples;
Trampling underfoot the pieces of silver;
He has scattered the peoples who delight in war.
AMP.31:
Princes and envoys shall come from Egypt;
Ethiopia will quickly stretch out her hands [with the offerings of submission] to God.
AMP.32:
Sing to God, O kingdoms of the earth,
Sing praises to the Lord! Selah.
AMP.33:
To Him who rides in the highest heavens, the ancient heavens,
Behold, He sends out His voice, a mighty and majestic voice.
AMP.34:
Ascribe strength to God;
His majesty is over Israel
And His strength is in the skies.
AMP.35:
O God, You are awesome and profoundly majestic from Your sanctuary;
The God of Israel gives strength and power to His people.
Blessed be God!

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Up with God!
Down with his enemies!
Adversaries, run for the hills!
MSG.2:
Gone like a puff of smoke,
like a blob of wax in the fire—
one look at God and the wicked vanish.
MSG.3:
When the righteous see God in action
they'll laugh, they'll sing,
they'll laugh and sing for joy.
MSG.4:
Sing hymns to God;
all heaven, sing out;
clear the way for the coming of Cloud-Rider.
Enjoy God,
cheer when you see him!
MSG.5:
Father of orphans,
champion of widows,
is God in his holy house.
MSG.6:
God makes homes for the homeless,
leads prisoners to freedom,
but leaves rebels to rot in hell.
MSG.7:
God, when you took the lead with your people,
when you marched out into the wild,
MSG.8:
Earth shook, sky broke out in a sweat;
God was on the march.
Even Sinai trembled at the sight of God on the move,
at the sight of Israel's God.
MSG.9:
You pour out rain in buckets, O God;
thorn and cactus become an oasis
MSG.10:
For your people to camp in and enjoy.
You set them up in business;
they went from rags to riches.
MSG.11:
The Lord gave the word;
thousands called out the good news:
MSG.12:
"Kings of the armies
are on the run, on the run!"
MSG.13:
While housewives, safe and sound back home,
divide up the plunder,
the plunder of Canaanite silver and gold.
MSG.14:
On that day that Shaddai scattered the kings,
snow fell on Black Mountain.
MSG.15:
You huge mountains, Bashan mountains,
mighty mountains, dragon mountains.
MSG.16:
All you mountains not chosen,
sulk now, and feel sorry for yourselves,
For this is the mountain God has chosen to live on;
he'll rule from this mountain forever.
MSG.17:
The chariots of God, twice ten thousand,
and thousands more besides,
The Lord in the lead, riding down Sinai—
straight to the Holy Place!
MSG.18:
You climbed to the High Place, captives in tow,
your arms full of booty from rebels,
And now you sit there in state,
God, sovereign God!
MSG.19:
Blessed be the Lord—
day after day he carries us along.
MSG.20:
He's our Savior, our God, oh yes!
He's God-for-us, he's God-who-saves-us.
Lord God knows all
death's ins and outs.
MSG.21:
What's more, he made heads roll,
split the skulls of the enemy
As he marched out of heaven,
MSG.22:
saying, "I tied up the Dragon in knots,
put a muzzle on the Deep Blue Sea."
MSG.23:
You can wade through your enemies' blood,
and your dogs taste of your enemies from your boots.
MSG.24:
See God on parade
to the sanctuary, my God,
my King on the march!
MSG.25:
Singers out front, the band behind,
maidens in the middle with castanets.
MSG.26:
The whole choir blesses God.
Like a fountain of praise, Israel blesses God.
MSG.27:
Look—little Benjamin's out
front and leading
Princes of Judah in their royal robes,
princes of Zebulon, princes of Naphtali.
MSG.28:
Parade your power, O God,
the power, O God, that made us what we are.
MSG.29:
Your temple, High God, is Jerusalem;
kings bring gifts to you.
MSG.30:
Rebuke that old crocodile, Egypt,
with her herd of wild bulls and calves,
Rapacious in her lust for silver,
crushing peoples, spoiling for a fight.
MSG.31:
Let Egyptian traders bring blue cloth
and Cush come running to God, her hands outstretched.
MSG.32:
Sing, O kings of the earth!
Sing praises to the Lord!
MSG.33:
There he is: Sky-Rider,
striding the ancient skies.
Listen—he's calling in thunder,
rumbling, rolling thunder.
MSG.34:
Call out "Bravo!" to God,
the High God of Israel.
His splendor and strength
rise huge as thunderheads.
MSG.35:
A terrible beauty, O God,
streams from your sanctuary.
It's Israel's strong God! He gives
power and might to his people!
O you, his people—bless God!

NIV.1:
Psalm 68
For the director of music. Of David. A psalm. A song.
May God arise, may his enemies be scattered;
may his foes flee before him.
NIV.2:
As smoke is blown away by the wind,
may you blow them away;
as wax melts before the fire,
may the wicked perish before God.
NIV.3:
But may the righteous be glad
and rejoice before God;
may they be happy and joyful.
NIV.4:
Sing to God, sing praise to his name,
extol him who rides on the clouds—
his name is the Lord—
and rejoice before him.
NIV.5:
A father to the fatherless, a defender of widows,
is God in his holy dwelling.
NIV.6:
God sets the lonely in families,
he leads forth the prisoners with singing;
but the rebellious live in a sun-scorched land.
NIV.7:
When you went out before your people, O God,
when you marched through the wasteland, Selah
NIV.8:
the earth shook,
the heavens poured down rain,
before God, the One of Sinai,
before God, the God of Israel.
NIV.9:
You gave abundant showers, O God;
you refreshed your weary inheritance.
NIV.10:
Your people settled in it,
and from your bounty, O God, you provided for the poor.
NIV.11:
The Lord announced the word,
and great was the company of those who proclaimed it:
NIV.12:
“Kings and armies flee in haste;
in the camps men divide the plunder.
NIV.13:
Even while you sleep among the campfires,
the wings of my dove are sheathed with silver,
its feathers with shining gold.”
NIV.14:
When the Almighty scattered the kings in the land,
it was like snow fallen on Zalmon.
NIV.15:
The mountains of Bashan are majestic mountains;
rugged are the mountains of Bashan.
NIV.16:
Why gaze in envy, O rugged mountains,
at the mountain where God chooses to reign,
where the Lord himself will dwell forever?
NIV.17:
The chariots of God are tens of thousands
and thousands of thousands;
the Lord has come from Sinai into his sanctuary.
NIV.18:
When you ascended on high,
you led captives in your train;
you received gifts from men,
even from the rebellious—
that you, O Lord God, might dwell there.
NIV.19:
Praise be to the Lord, to God our Savior,
who daily bears our burdens. Selah
NIV.20:
Our God is a God who saves;
from the Sovereign Lord comes escape from death.
NIV.21:
Surely God will crush the heads of his enemies,
the hairy crowns of those who go on in their sins.
NIV.22:
The Lord says, “I will bring them from Bashan;
I will bring them from the depths of the sea,
NIV.23:
that you may plunge your feet in the blood of your foes,
while the tongues of your dogs have their share.”
NIV.24:
Your procession has come into view, O God,
the procession of my God and King into the sanctuary.
NIV.25:
In front are the singers, after them the musicians;
with them are the maidens playing tambourines.
NIV.26:
Praise God in the great congregation;
praise the Lord in the assembly of Israel.
NIV.27:
There is the little tribe of Benjamin, leading them,
there the great throng of Judah’s princes,
and there the princes of Zebulun and of Naphtali.
NIV.28:
Summon your power, O God;
show us your strength, O God, as you have done before.
NIV.29:
Because of your temple at Jerusalem
kings will bring you gifts.
NIV.30:
Rebuke the beast among the reeds,
the herd of bulls among the calves of the nations.
Humbled, may it bring bars of silver.
Scatter the nations who delight in war.
NIV.31:
Envoys will come from Egypt;
Cush will submit herself to God.
NIV.32:
Sing to God, O kingdoms of the earth,
sing praise to the Lord, Selah
NIV.33:
to him who rides the ancient skies above,
who thunders with mighty voice.
NIV.34:
Proclaim the power of God,
whose majesty is over Israel,
whose power is in the skies.
NIV.35:
You are awesome, O God, in your sanctuary;
the God of Israel gives power and strength to his people.
Praise be to God!

NRSVCE.1:
Let God rise up, let his enemies be scattered;
let those who hate him flee before him.
NRSVCE.2:
As smoke is driven away, so drive them away;
as wax melts before the fire,
let the wicked perish before God.
NRSVCE.3:
But let the righteous be joyful;
let them exult before God;
let them be jubilant with joy.
NRSVCE.4:
Sing to God, sing praises to his name;
lift up a song to him who rides upon the clouds —
his name is the LORD —
be exultant before him.
NRSVCE.5:
Father of orphans and protector of widows
is God in his holy habitation.
NRSVCE.6:
God gives the desolate a home to live in;
he leads out the prisoners to prosperity,
but the rebellious live in a parched land.
NRSVCE.7:
O God, when you went out before your people,
when you marched through the wilderness, Selah
NRSVCE.8:
the earth quaked, the heavens poured down rain
at the presence of God, the God of Sinai,
at the presence of God, the God of Israel.
NRSVCE.9:
Rain in abundance, O God, you showered abroad;
you restored your heritage when it languished;
NRSVCE.10:
your flock found a dwelling in it;
in your goodness, O God, you provided for the needy.
NRSVCE.11:
The Lord gives the command;
great is the company of those who bore the tidings:
NRSVCE.12:
“The kings of the armies, they flee, they flee!”
The women at home divide the spoil,
NRSVCE.13:
though they stay among the sheepfolds —
the wings of a dove covered with silver,
its pinions with green gold.
NRSVCE.14:
When the Almighty scattered kings there,
snow fell on Zalmon.
NRSVCE.15:
O mighty mountain, mountain of Bashan;
O many-peaked mountain, mountain of Bashan!
NRSVCE.16:
Why do you look with envy, O many-peaked mountain,
at the mount that God desired for his abode,
where the LORD will reside forever?
NRSVCE.17:
With mighty chariotry, twice ten thousand,
thousands upon thousands,
the Lord came from Sinai into the holy place.
NRSVCE.18:
You ascended the high mount,
leading captives in your train
and receiving gifts from people,
even from those who rebel against the LORD God’s abiding there.
NRSVCE.19:
Blessed be the Lord,
who daily bears us up;
God is our salvation. Selah
NRSVCE.20:
Our God is a God of salvation,
and to GOD, the Lord, belongs escape from death.
NRSVCE.21:
But God will shatter the heads of his enemies,
the hairy crown of those who walk in their guilty ways.
NRSVCE.22:
The Lord said,
“I will bring them back from Bashan,
I will bring them back from the depths of the sea,
NRSVCE.23:
so that you may bathe your feet in blood,
so that the tongues of your dogs may have their share from the foe.”
NRSVCE.24:
Your solemn processions are seen, O God,
the processions of my God, my King, into the sanctuary —
NRSVCE.25:
the singers in front, the musicians last,
between them girls playing tambourines:
NRSVCE.26:
“Bless God in the great congregation,
the LORD, O you who are of Israel’s fountain!”
NRSVCE.27:
There is Benjamin, the least of them, in the lead,
the princes of Judah in a body,
the princes of Zebulun, the princes of Naphtali.
NRSVCE.28:
Summon your might, O God;
show your strength, O God, as you have done for us before.
NRSVCE.29:
Because of your temple at Jerusalem
kings bear gifts to you.
NRSVCE.30:
Rebuke the wild animals that live among the reeds,
the herd of bulls with the calves of the peoples.
Trample under foot those who lust after tribute;
scatter the peoples who delight in war.
NRSVCE.31:
Let bronze be brought from Egypt;
let Ethiopia hasten to stretch out its hands to God.
NRSVCE.32:
Sing to God, O kingdoms of the earth;
sing praises to the Lord, Selah
NRSVCE.33:
O rider in the heavens, the ancient heavens;
listen, he sends out his voice, his mighty voice.
NRSVCE.34:
Ascribe power to God,
whose majesty is over Israel;
and whose power is in the skies.
NRSVCE.35:
Awesome is God in his sanctuary,
the God of Israel;
he gives power and strength to his people.
Blessed be God!

NET.1: God springs into action! His enemies scatter; his adversaries run from him.
NET.2: As smoke is driven away by the wind, so you drive them away. As wax melts before fire, so the wicked are destroyed before God.
NET.3: But the godly are happy; they rejoice before God and are overcome with joy.
NET.4: Sing to God! Sing praises to his name! Exalt the one who rides on the clouds! For the LORD is his name! Rejoice before him!
NET.5: He is a father to the fatherless and an advocate for widows. God rules from his holy palace.
NET.6: God settles those who have been deserted in their own homes; he frees prisoners and grants them prosperity. But sinful rebels live in the desert.
NET.7: O God, when you lead your people into battle, when you march through the desert, (Selah)
NET.8: the earth shakes, yes, the heavens pour down rain before God, the God of Sinai, before God, the God of Israel.
NET.9: O God, you cause abundant showers to fall on your chosen people. When they are tired, you sustain them,
NET.10: for you live among them. You sustain the oppressed with your good blessings, O God.
NET.11: The Lord speaks; many, many women spread the good news.
NET.12: Kings leading armies run away — they run away! The lovely lady of the house divides up the loot.
NET.13: When you lie down among the sheepfolds, the wings of the dove are covered with silver and with glittering gold.
NET.14: When the sovereign judge scatters kings, let it snow on Zalmon!
NET.15: The mountain of Bashan is a towering mountain; the mountain of Bashan is a mountain with many peaks.
NET.16: Why do you look with envy, O mountains with many peaks, at the mountain where God has decided to live? Indeed the LORD will live there permanently!
NET.17: God has countless chariots; they number in the thousands. The Lord comes from Sinai in holy splendor.
NET.18: You ascend on high, you have taken many captives. You receive tribute from men, including even sinful rebels. Indeed the LORD God lives there!
NET.19: The Lord deserves praise! Day after day he carries our burden, the God who delivers us. (Selah)
NET.20: Our God is a God who delivers; the LORD, the sovereign Lord, can rescue from death.
NET.21: Indeed God strikes the heads of his enemies, the hairy foreheads of those who persist in rebellion.
NET.22: The Lord says, “I will retrieve them from Bashan, I will bring them back from the depths of the sea,
NET.23: so that your feet may stomp in their blood, and your dogs may eat their portion of the enemies’ corpses.”
NET.24: They see your processions, O God — the processions of my God, my king, who marches along in holy splendor.
NET.25: Singers walk in front; musicians follow playing their stringed instruments, in the midst of young women playing tambourines.
NET.26: In your large assemblies praise God, the LORD, in the assemblies of Israel!
NET.27: There is little Benjamin, their ruler, and the princes of Judah in their robes, along with the princes of Zebulun and the princes of Naphtali.
NET.28: God has decreed that you will be powerful. O God, you who have acted on our behalf, demonstrate your power,
NET.29: as you come out of your temple in Jerusalem! Kings bring tribute to you.
NET.30: Sound your battle cry against the wild beast of the reeds, and the nations that assemble like a herd of calves led by bulls! They humble themselves and offer gold and silver as tribute. God scatters the nations that like to do battle.
NET.31: They come with red cloth from Egypt, Ethiopia voluntarily offers tribute to God.
NET.32: O kingdoms of the earth, sing to God! Sing praises to the Lord, (Selah)
NET.33: to the one who rides through the sky from ancient times! Look! He thunders loudly.
NET.34: Acknowledge God’s power, his sovereignty over Israel, and the power he reveals in the skies!
NET.35: You are awe-inspiring, O God, as you emerge from your holy temple! It is the God of Israel who gives the people power and strength. God deserves praise!
